meaning: phá vỡ sự ngại ngùng, bắt đầu trò chuyện
meaningEn: to do or say something to relieve tension or get a conversation going
example: He told a joke to break the ice at the meeting.

meaningEn: something very easy to do
example: The exam was a piece of cake.

meaningEn: to study hard
example: I have to hit the books this weekend before the test.

meaningEn: feeling slightly ill
example: She's feeling a bit under the weather today.

meaning: hiếm khi, thỉnh thoảng lắm mới
meaningEn: very rarely
example: We only go to the cinema once in a blue moon.

phrase: the ball is in your court
meaning: đến lượt bạn quyết định/hành động
meaningEn: it is up to you to make the next decision or step
example: I've made my offer, so the ball is in your court now.

phrase: cost an arm and a leg
meaningEn: to be very expensive
example: That new phone costs an arm and a leg.

phrase: bite off more than you can chew
meaningEn: to take on more than you can handle
example: By taking three jobs, he bit off more than he could chew.

meaningEn: to become difficult to control
example: The party got out of hand after midnight.

meaning: dừng công việc lại (cho hôm nay)
meaningEn: to stop working on something for the day
example: We've done enough — let's call it a day.

meaning: cùng quan điểm, hiểu giống nhau
meaningEn: to share the same understanding or opinion
example: Let's make sure we're all on the same page before we start.

meaningEn: said when a person you were just talking about appears
example: Speak of the devil — here comes John now!

meaningEn: to stop trying to do something
example: Don't give up — you're almost there!

meaningEn: to feel excited about something that will happen
example: I look forward to meeting you next week.

meaningEn: to discover information
example: I need to find out what time the train leaves.

meaningEn: to continue doing something
example: Please carry on with your work.

meaningEn: to postpone or delay something
example: We had to put off the meeting until Friday.

meaningEn: to have no more of something left
example: We've run out of milk.

meaning: hòa hợp, hợp tính với ai
meaningEn: to have a good relationship with someone
example: She gets along with all her colleagues.

meaningEn: to take care of someone or something
example: Can you look after my dog this weekend?

meaningEn: to think of an idea or plan
example: She came up with a brilliant solution.

meaningEn: to reject an offer; or to reduce volume
example: He turned down the job offer.

meaningEn: to arrange or establish something
example: They set up a new company last year.

meaningEn: to mention a topic; or to raise a child
example: She brought up an interesting point in the discussion.
